(21) Learning Hexo from Scratch - NexT Theme: What Else Can You Configure?
This is the last post in the NexT theme chapter, so let's cover a few more configuration options.
Out of the box, your home page shows the full content of every post. NexT has three ways to add a "Read more" / "More" button instead:
First option — manual cut. Drop this in your article where you want the truncation:
, for auto-cut you also need to install
Third option — and the one I use. Just add a

---
title: My First Blog Post
date: 2020-07-18 14:10:24
tags:
description:
---
When the home page renders, it'll use the
you provided and hide the rest of the content:
Personally I'm not a fan of the "Powered by" line, so I usually turn it off. Search for
That said, leaving it on is a small way of respecting the original authors — I just don't personally like it. Since the option exists officially, I'll mention it but won't push either way.
Above the "Powered by" line there's a
field. Personally I want anyone reposting my articles to include a link back to the original, so I write:

# If not defined, `author` from Hexo `_config.yml` will be used.
copyright: If reposting, please keep the original author name and source link.
# If not defined, `author` from Hexo `_config.yml` will be used.
If reposting, please keep the original author name and source link.
(Even though my articles are mostly just notes, it's still worth respecting the original. When I write notes I also credit the articles I'm referencing.)
You can also change the icon that appears in front of the copyright line (default is a heart), and add animation to it. Search for
Icon between year and copyright info.
to find the config. Here's an example of swapping the icon and turning animation on:

icon:
  # Icon name in Font Awesome. See: https://fontawesome.com/icons
  name: fab fa-accessible-icon
  # If you want to animate the icon, set it to true.
  animated: true
  # Change the color of icon, using Hex Code.
  color: "#ff0000"
# Icon name in Font Awesome. See: https://fontawesome.com/icons
# If you want to animate the icon, set it to true.
# Change the color of icon, using Hex Code.
You should see a new, animated icon. I'll leave it for you to try yourself.
Last one — avatar. The sidebar avatar is off by default. Search for
and paste in your image URL.
. The first makes the avatar circular; the second makes it rotate when the cursor hovers over it. Personally I prefer the circle look; the rotation just makes the avatar spin once on hover.

# Sidebar Avatar
avatar:
  # Replace the default image and set the url here.
  url: /images/avatar.gif
  # If true, the avatar will be dispalyed in circle.
  rounded: true
  # If true, the avatar will be rotated with the cursor.
  rotated: true
# Replace the default image and set the url here.
# If true, the avatar will be dispalyed in circle.
# If true, the avatar will be rotated with the cursor.
of other configurable bits — I only covered what I've tweaked on my own setup. That's it for today's Ironman post. See you in the next one
